title: Aggregation, Grouping, HAVING, and Honest Metrics · SQL Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A service lead needs ticket counts and response effort by team without double counting or hiding teams with no work. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an audited service-metrics query pack. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, and MAX while predicting empty-set, NULL, and type behavior.
Set result grain with GROUP BY and verify that every selected value is grouped or aggregated.
Separate row filtering in WHERE from group filtering in HAVING.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Aggregate Functions · changed case · Quiz Aggregate Functions
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Report every team with ticket count, unresolved count, and total event minutes. Team Drift has no tickets and ticket 101 has 6 events; predict result grain and prove joins do not multiply either measure. Competency probe: Use COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, and MAX while predicting empty-set, NULL, and type behavior.

Which action demonstrates Aggregate Functions without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Aggregate Functions · changed case · Quiz Aggregate Functions.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use count, sum, avg, min, and max while predicting empty-set, null, and type behavior. For Aggregate Functions · changed case · Quiz Aggregate Functions, keep Aggregate Functions connected to executable relational evidence.
